text.tspan: Actual and imputed rents as share of GDP vs. GDP per capita, 1959 to
Imputed rent is an estimate of the value of housing services received by home owners in living in their own property,
equivalent to what they would have to have to pay to rent a similar property. GDP per capita is adjusted to account for inflation
Actual and imputed rents as share of GDP
Note: Rent share of GDP uses GDP calculated from the expenditure approach, using OECD data. GDP per capita is taken from Penn World
